    Building Automated Docker Image in Docker Hub with Jenkins and Packer

    1 minute read

    Published: April 14, 2018

    Docker is a tool for building application image and packer is a tool for creating golden image from single source configuration. So docker images can be built from both docker and packer. If you bake them with Jenkins then application image creation and uploading process to docker image repository will be automated.

    If you want to build application image using Docker then you must write a Dockerfile. You have to keep special concern for minimizing the layers in Dockerfile. Because, more the layers the more option to reverse engineer the image. Using docker history command we can reverse engineer the steps of Dockerfile from a docker image:

    $ docker history $IMAGE_ID
$ docker history $IMAGE_ID --no-trunc

    In order to build docker image with packer you need packer and docker installed under a user with sudo privileges. You can follow the template here. Before you try to build application image you must login to docker hub using docker login command once.

    $ git clone https://github.com/shudarshon/infrastructure-as-a-code.git
$ cd infrastructure-as-a-code/packer/docker
$ make validate
$ make build

    If you want to automate the application packaging process then you can use the command in a Jenkins pipeline. Since you are also minimizing docker layers to a single step in docker history command so it is also secure. At last, remove all docker artifacts after packaging process:

    $ docker system prune -af

    This post is focused on Jenkins pipeline stages to implement automated application packaging using docker without Dockerfile!

    Tags: docker, iaac, jenkins, packer

    Using post-mortem report as a medium to build trust across cross-team members

    2 minute read

    Published: October 17, 2020

    We know that “post-mortem” is a term that is widely used in various criminal investigations. By analyzing the various subjects of crime and mentioning them in the “Post-mortem report” helps the mystery of crime to be revealed by conducting a thorough investigation on them. Today we will discuss how this “post-mortem report” plays an important role in modern software development as a medium of better communication across cross-team members.

    3 minute read

    Published: August 09, 2019

    Writing incident postmortem to learn from previous history and avoid blaming

    3 minute read

    Published: July 31, 2019

    Errors are sneaky, often remain hidden, smart and somehow get past no matter they are caused by any human action or any prevailing bug. This error often causes incidents in the running system causing a great negative impact on the team. To avoid the same mistakes in the future it is needed to document them properly which is often referred to as incident postmortem writing.
